description:
Fourth and terminal implementation phase of bl-f021. Bumps `min_bl_version` on the project's `balls/tasks` orphan branch so that pre-revision `bl` binaries cannot operate against the migrated tracker. Depends on Phase 3 (bl-05e5) — the migration must be discoverable from `bl prime` *and* widely deployed before the version floor is raised.

This is the coordination ball, not the code ball. The implementation is a one-field edit on `project.json`. The work is the social check that everyone who uses any clone of any participating repo has upgraded.

## Scope

Per SPEC-clone-layout.md §12 (the "pre-revision bl on a post-three-file clone" row) and the epic's "phases" section: "min_bl_version bump on the orphan branch project.json, after teams have upgraded."

### Implements

1. **Bump `min_bl_version` on `project.json`** to the version of `bl` that introduced the three-file layout (Phase 1's release version — to be filled in when Phase 1 ships, recorded as a note on this ball at that time).

2. **`bl prime` warning channel asserts** when `min_bl_version` > `current`. This is already gated by SPEC-clone-layout §14.24 — Phase 1 must implement the warning; Phase 4 just lights it up by setting the field. Verify §14.24 still passes against the bumped value.

3. **Release ceremony** — the bump lands as a state-branch commit on the project's tracker. No code change needed; the ball is `bl create` → notes recording the soak/coordination checks → state-branch commit → close.

### Soak/coordination checklist

Filled in incrementally as Phase 3 (bl-05e5) bedds in. Each row records the check + the date it cleared:

- [ ] All known dogfood clones (this repo + balls-github-plugin) report no legacy markers from `bl doctor`.
- [ ] All open balls referencing legacy layout (`.balls/config.json`, `workspace.json`, `delivery`, `pre_check`) are closed or migrated.
- [ ] `bl prime --migrate` has been the default for at least one minor-release cycle (the default-flip date recorded on bl-05e5 is the start).
- [ ] No issues filed naming the legacy layout in the last 30 days.
- [ ] Release notes for the version that ships the bump call it out explicitly (`bl < X.Y.Z` will refuse to operate on this project's tracker after this release).

### Explicitly NOT in scope

- Removal of dual-read code paths from `Store::discover` — defer to a follow-up cleanup ball after Phase 4 has been live for a documented period. The dual-read is cheap and asymmetric: keeping it costs ~one branch in discover; removing it forecloses the only graceful path back from a misrouted older binary.
- Any code change to `bl`. If a code change is needed during Phase 4, that signals the soak wasn't enough — file a follow-up rather than expanding this ball's scope.

## Conformance tests gated by this ball

SPEC-clone-layout §14 test 24 already covers the warning. Phase 4 adds:
- A regression check that the bumped `min_bl_version` matches the release-tagged version of `bl` that introduced the three-file layout. If they drift (e.g. someone bumped `min_bl_version` but the release version is older), CI catches the mismatch before publish.

## Why a discrete ball, not a postscript on Phase 3

The bump is a contract change between every clone and the tracker — once landed, older `bl` binaries are locked out. That deserves its own audit trail with its own soak checklist visible in the ball description. Bundling it into Phase 3 would hide the coordination work in implementation notes and remove the natural pre-bump checkpoint where the team agrees "yes, now."

## Risks

- A clone using older `bl` that hasn't fetched the tracker since the bump won't see the new `min_bl_version` until next prime, then the warning fires. There's a small window where the older `bl` operates on stale state; this is documented in SPEC-clone-layout §12 as the accepted "documented-but-not-engineered" caveat. Mitigation is the soak + release-notes call-out, not engineering.
- Versioning hygiene: the bumped value must reference an actually-released version. The CI check above is the gate. If Phase 1's release version is unknown when this ball is filed, that's fine — the ball stays open until the version is recorded.
title: Phase 4: min_bl_version bump on the orphan tracker
